title: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie

seo.title: Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma: Authentic Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma at home! This recipe brings authentic Middle Eastern spices to your rotisserie for juicy, flavorful chicken. Get the recipe!
longTailKeywords: how to make rotisserie chicken shawarma
authentic Middle Eastern rotisserie chicken shawarma
h2Headings: Tips for Perfect Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma
seo.imageAlt: A whole rotisserie chicken seasoned with shawarma spices, golden brown and ready to be carved.
seo.imageCaption: Golden-brown Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma, perfectly spiced and ready for your next Middle Eastern feast!

description: Experience the beloved flavors of Middle Eastern shawarma with the convenience of your home rotisserie. This recipe transforms a whole chicken into a succulent, spice-infused masterpiece, perfect for a family feast. Its golden-crisp skin and tender, aromatic meat will transport your taste buds straight to the streets of Beirut or Amman.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the whole chicken dry thoroughly with paper towels. This helps the skin crisp up beautifully. Remove any excess fat or loose skin.

instructions.title: Make the Shawarma Marinade
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, turmeric powder, ground cardamom, ground cinnamon, black pepper, cayenne pepper (if using), and salt. Mix well to form a thick paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Generously rub the shawarma marinade all over the chicken, ensuring to get some under the skin, especially on the breast and thighs. For best results, cover the chicken and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or preferably overnight, allowing the flavors to deeply penetrate.

instructions.title: Prepare for Rotisserie
instructions.text: Preheat your grill or oven with a rotisserie attachment to medium-high heat (around 375-400F or 190-200C). Securely truss the chicken with kitchen twine to keep its shape during cooking. This prevents wings and legs from flopping and ensures even cooking. Skewer the chicken onto the rotisserie rod.

instructions.title: Rotisserie Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the rotisserie rod with the chicken onto the rotisserie motor. Place a drip pan underneath to catch juices. Cook for approximately 35-45 minutes per pound, or until the internal temperature reaches 165F (74C) in the thickest part of the thigh, without touching the bone. The skin should be deeply golden and crispy.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the rotisserie and transfer it to a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let it rest for 10-15 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and flavorful chicken. Carve and serve with warm pita bread, tahini sauce, pickled turnips, and fresh vegetables.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, add a tablespoon of grated onion or a pinch of sumac to the marinade.
Do not skip the trussing step; it is crucial for even cooking and a beautifully shaped rotisserie chicken.
Serve your Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma in warm pita pockets with fresh lettuce, sliced tomatoes, cucumbers, pickled vegetables, and a generous drizzle of homemade tahini sauce.
article: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie is a beloved Middle Eastern Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
